This market will resolve according to the player who is awarded the 2025–26 NBA Sixth Man of the Year.

If the listed player is not announced as a finalist for the 2025–26 Sixth Man of the Year, this market will resolve to "No".

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the NBA. However, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on the NBA Sixth Man of the Year reflects a neck-and-neck battle among bench standouts, with Keldon Johnson's 27.8% implied probability edging Jonathan Kuminga's 25.1% amid surging Spurs and Warriors production. Johnson's recent 20+ point outings off the bench have boosted his case, mirroring Kuminga's efficient scoring and rebounding in Golden State's injury-riddled rotation. Reed Sheppard's rookie spark for Houston (17.1%) and Naz Reid's defending-champ consistency (17.0%) keep the pack tight, as no candidate dominates in bench points per game or win shares—voters often split on volume scorers versus efficiency in high-impact roles. Team playoff pushes and hot streaks could tip the scales late.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus crowns Detroit Pistons center Jalen Duren as the clear frontrunner for NBA Most Improved Player at 49.3%, propelled by his explosive year-over-year surge to career-best averages of 14.2 points, 12.8 rebounds, and 2.5 assists through early 2025, anchoring the Pistons' surprising playoff push amid Cade Cunningham's absence. Washington Wizards forward Deni Avdija trails at 28.1%, buoyed by his post-trade breakout to 21.2 points, 8.5 rebounds, and elite two-way defense, though voter fatigue from smaller-market play tempers enthusiasm. Atlanta Hawks wing Jalen Johnson (7.8%) gains from doubled scoring output and a max extension, but Duren's volume rebounding dominance and team uplift solidify his edge, with odds reflecting recent form amid voter emphasis on raw stat leaps over efficiency.

This market will resolve according to the player that finishes the 2025–26 NBA regular season with the highest points per-game average of any qualified player.
